title: Find perimeter when a side length is missing
description: Find perimeter of polygons when not all side lengths are labeled.
topic_path: Module 7: Geometry and measurement word problems
Topic C: Problem solving with perimeter
Find perimeter when a side length is missing

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of rectangles.  

**Label the missing side length.**

[[☃ label-image 1]]


**What is the perimeter of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\text{m}$
hints:
First, let's find the missing side length.
We can split the figure into rectangles to help us figure out the missing length.



[[☃ image 1]]

Opposite sides of a rectangle have the same length.



[[☃ image 1]]

The missing side length is $\greenD{2\text{ m}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

Now, let's find the perimeter of the figure.

To find the perimeter, we add all of the side lengths.

$\begin{align} \text{Perimeter} &= 7 + 5 + \greenD{2} + 3 + {3} + 4 + 2 + 6
\\\\
&=32\text { m} \end{align}$

Here is the completed figure:



[[☃ image 1]]

The perimeter is $32$ meters.

options.imageAlt: An eight-sided figure, with side lengths starting from the top right: 5 meters to the left, 7 meters down, 6 meters to the right, 2 meters up, 4 meters left, 3 meters up, 3 meters right, and an unlabeled side length up to the starting point.

answers.message: One of the lengths is missing.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of a square and a rectangle.  



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the perimeter of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: First, let's find the missing side lengths.

Opposite sides of a rectangle have the same length.  All sides of a square are the same length.



[[☃ image 1]]

One of those lengths is *inside* the figure.  It is **not** part of the perimeter.

Add all of the *outside* side lengths together to find the perimeter.

$\begin{align}\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{5} + \redC{5} + \tealD{8} + \goldE{5} + \maroonC{8} + \blueD{5}\\\\
&=36 \text { cm}\end{align}$
The perimeter is $36\,\text{cm}$.

options.alt: A rectangle made up of a square and a rectangle. The square has a height of 5 centimeters. The rectangle has a height of 5 centimeters and a base of 8 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of a square and a rectangle.  



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the perimeter of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: First, let's find the missing side lengths.

Opposite sides of a rectangle have the same length.  All sides of a square are the same length.



[[☃ image 1]]

One of those lengths is *inside* the figure.  It is **not** part of the perimeter.

Add all of the *outside* side lengths together to find the perimeter.

$\begin{align}\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{4} + \redC{4} + \tealD{10} + \goldE{4} + \maroonC{10} + \blueD{4}\\\\
&=36 \text { cm}\end{align}$
The perimeter is $36\,\text{cm}$.

options.alt: A rectangle made up of a square and a rectangle. The square has a height of 4 centimeters. The rectangle has a height of 4 centimeters and a base of 10 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of a triangle and a rectangle.  All of the sides of the triangle are the same length.



[[☃ image 1]]



**What is the perimeter of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: First, let's find the missing side lengths.

Opposite sides of a rectangle have the same length.  All sides of the triangle are the same length.


[[☃ image 1]]

One of those lengths is *inside* the figure.  It is **not** part of the perimeter.

Add all of the *outside* side lengths together to find the perimeter.

$\begin{align}\text{Perimeter} &= \blueD{3} +  \greenD{3} + \purpleC{11} +  \tealD{3} + \goldE{11}\\\\
&=31 \text { cm}\end{align}$
The perimeter is $31\,\text{cm}$.

options.alt: A figure made up of a triangle and a rectangle. The triangle has a side length of 3 centimeters. The rectangle has a height of 3 centimeters and a base of 11 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of a triangle and a rectangle.  All of the sides of the triangle are the same length.


[[☃ image 1]]



**What is the perimeter of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: First, let's find the missing side lengths.

Opposite sides of a rectangle have the same length.  All sides of the triangle are the same length.



[[☃ image 1]]

One of those lengths is *inside* the figure.  It is **not** part of the perimeter.

Add all of the *outside* side lengths together to find the perimeter.

$\begin{align}\text{Perimeter} &= \blueD{5} +  \greenD{5} + \purpleC{23} +  \tealD{5} + \goldE{23}\\\\
&=61\text { cm}\end{align}$
The perimeter is $61\text{ cm}$.

options.alt: A figure made up of a triangle and a rectangle. The triangle has a side length of 5 centimeters. The rectangle has a height of 5 centimeters and a base of 23 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of $3$ rectangles that are all the same size.  



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the perimeter of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: First, let's find the missing side lengths.

Opposite sides of a rectangle have the same length.  All of the rectangles are the same size.


[[☃ image 1]]

Two of those lengths are *inside* the figure.  They are **not** part of the perimeter.

Add all of the *outside* side lengths together to find the perimeter.

$\begin{align}\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{27} + \redC{14} + \tealD{14} + \color{#E8C11C}{14} +27 + \purpleA{14}+ \maroonC{14} + \blueD{14}\\\\
&=138 \text { cm}\end{align}$
The perimeter is $138\,\text{cm}$.

options.alt: A rectangle made up of three identical rectangles. The first rectangle has a labeled height of 27 centimeters and a labeled base of 14 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure is made of $3$ rectangles that are all the same size.  



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the perimeter of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints: First, let's find the missing side lengths.

Opposite sides of a rectangle have the same length.  All of the rectangles are the same size.



[[☃ image 1]]

Two of those lengths are *inside* the figure.  They are **not** part of the perimeter.

Add all of the *outside* side lengths together to find the perimeter.

$\begin{align}\text{Perimeter} &= \greenD{7} + \redC{12} + \tealD{12} + \color{#E8C11C}{12} +7 + \purpleA{12}+ \maroonC{12} + \blueD{12}\\\\
&=86\text { cm}\end{align}$
The perimeter is $86\text{ cm}$.

options.alt: A rectangle made up of three identical rectangles. The first rectangle has a labeled height of 7 centimeters and a labeled base of 12 centimeters.
